title: agalinis pseudaphylla
intro: Agalinis pseudaphylla, also known as Downy Agalinis or False Foxglove, is a flowering plant species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including soothing digestive issues and alleviating menstrual cramps. Agalinis pseudaphylla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation, ease respiratory discomfort, and support immune function.
